name.value: Chiang Mai Green Valley Country Club

prose.overview: Chiang Mai Green Valley Country Club is an 18-hole, par-72 golf course in the Mae Rim district north of Chiang Mai city, not to be confused with the nearby Summit Green Valley Chiangmai Country Club, which shares a similar name and general location. Chiang Mai Green Valley is a separate facility with its own TripAdvisor profile and distinct course identity, though both properties are in the Mae Rim area and reportedly share a designer (Dennis Griffiths) and a founding era around 1990. The course is characterised by minimal tree cover relative to other Northern Thailand layouts, open fairways, extensive water hazards on nearly every hole, and numerous bunkers — a layout that demands shot-making accuracy throughout. The course draws primarily local Chiang Mai golfers and those visiting the Mae Rim valley, and is considered a good-value alternative to the higher-priced resort courses in the region. NOTE: Many key data fields for this course could not be confirmed via web research and require direct verification before publication.
prose.layout_and_experience:
Based on available review sources, the course presents an open, parkland character with minimal tree shelter — quite different from the densely forested mountain settings of the Gassan Group properties or the palm-lined Summit Green Valley. The exposed fairways mean that wind can influence shot selection on some holes, and without tree corridors to frame shots, course management requires discipline in club selection and target line.

Water hazards are a constant feature throughout the round, appearing on virtually every hole and making the layout more demanding than its green fee level might suggest. The bunker strategy is relatively straightforward, with sand traps positioned to catch errant approaches rather than tee shots. The greens are reported to be receptive but with subtle contours that reward accurate approach play.

The 18-hole layout stretches to approximately 7,200 yards from the back tees — a similar total distance to Summit Green Valley — and plays to par 72 with the standard combination of par-3s, par-4s, and par-5s. Because this course has less online documentation than other Chiang Mai courses, specific hole-by-hole character details are not available without visiting the course.
prose.tips: Given the limited online data for this course, contact the club directly before visiting to confirm current green fees, opening hours, and caddie/cart requirements. As a local-focused course in the Mae Rim area, it may offer more flexible or walk-on booking than the larger resort properties. The open setting means afternoon heat is felt more keenly here than at shaded mountain courses — early morning rounds are recommended during the hot season. Bring extra water and sun protection. The Mae Rim location makes it convenient to combine with other activities in the valley (elephant sanctuaries, craft villages) for a mixed-activity day.
prose.location_and_access: Chiang Mai Green Valley Country Club is located in the Mae Rim district, approximately 15–20 kilometres north of Chiang Mai city centre. Visitors from Bangkok fly to Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX), a journey of approximately 1 hour 10 minutes from Suvarnabhumi or Don Mueang — driving the 700 kilometres from Bangkok is not practical. The course is reachable by Grab taxi from Chiang Mai city along Route 107 north toward Mae Rim.
prose.rental_cta_context: Planning a round at Chiang Mai Green Valley Country Club on your Northern Thailand golf visit? Rent premium clubs in Bangkok — delivered to your hotel before you fly — so you arrive ready to play without worrying about airline baggage fees.
